word: H8512 - תֵּל אָבִיב - Têl ʼÂbîyb - tale aw-beeb' - Tel-abib

sections.paragraph:
Original: תּל אביב

Transliteration: têl 'âbı̂yb

Phonetic: tale aw-beeb'

BDB Definition:

Tel-abib = "mound of the flood"
  1. a city in Babylon, the home of the prophet Ezekiel, located on the river Chebar which was probably a branch of the Euphrates

Origin: from H8510 and H24

TWOT entry: None

Part(s) of speech: Proper Name Location
